frozen_components: Gated DeltaNet / Mamba-2 / RWKV-7 host model
architecture.summary: WriteSAE is a sparse autoencoder that learns rank-1 matrix atoms matching the shape of per-token writes in recurrent language-model caches (Gated DeltaNet, Mamba-2, RWKV-7). At inference, when the SAE activates an atom at a cache-write position, the model's native write is replaced with the learned atom scaled by its SAE coefficient, and the forward pass continues. The mechanism treats the recurrent state matrix as the target object, not the residual stream. Information flows from cache-write events → TopK SAE encoding → atom selection and scaling → cache substitution → downstream read (query-applied to modified cache) → final logit output.
trained_components: WriteSAE TopK sparse autoencoder (rank-1 decoder atoms)

biblio.title: WriteSAE: Sparse Autoencoders for Recurrent State

claims.dataset: held-out OpenWebText passages, Qwen3.5-0.8B Gated DeltaNet L9 H4
claims.metric: fraction of positions where atom beats deletion in KL divergence
claims.statement: At positions where the SAE activates an atom, the atom gives a closer final token distribution than deleting the write on 92.4% of evaluated positions; averaged per atom, the rate is 89.8%.
For Gated DeltaNet, a formula using the forget gate, read query, and output embedding predicts the resulting logit change with R² = 0.98.

claims.metric: fraction of positions where atom beats deletion
claims.statement: The same replacement test (atom beats deletion) transfers to Mamba-2-370M at 88.1%.

claims.dataset: Qwen3.5-0.8B L9 H4 generation probe, targets ranked 100–1000
claims.metric: target inclusion rate (token appears in 20-token greedy continuation)
claims.statement: In generation, writing the formula's chosen direction into three consecutive cache positions at 3× the norm of the model's write makes tokens initially ranked 100–1000 by the unmodified model appear in 100% of continuations, up from 33.3%.
Atoms split into two classes by cosine alignment to native writes: register atoms (median cosine 0.92) and bundle atoms (median cosine 0.02), separated by Gaussian mixture model with BIC support.
Boundary-feature amplification on Qwen3.5-4B-Base L9 reduces newlines per 400 generated tokens from 16.8 to 12.7 (24% reduction), with paired t-test p=0.01 and Cohen's d=0.59.

claims.dataset: Mamba-2-370M L24 H0 and Qwen3.5-4B L12 H8
claims.statement: The GDN logit-change formula does not transfer to Mamba-2 L24 H0 or Qwen3.5-4B L12 H8; on these models the same formula yields negative R² (−0.18 and −0.27 respectively). Architectures without the same multiplicative gate need their own coefficient form.

claims.metric: fraction of positions where atom beats deletion
claims.statement: At Qwen3.5-4B L12 H8 with the same SAE recipe used at 0.8B, atoms beat deleting the write only at chance level (50.1% pooled, Wilson CI [48.6%, 51.6%]). The 4B SAE achieves better validation MSE than the 0.8B SAE, so the failure is not due to under-training; reconstruction and substitution objectives decouple at scale.
